content: Just as human language learners explicitly build reflexion (reflection) as a metacognitive vocabulary and habit to deepen learning in high school literacy curricula, large language models can be fine-tuned to internalize a 'vocabulary of self-reflexion' structured after second-language acquisition principles to improve their self-reflection mechanism performance. This framing reframes LLM self-reflection not as a purely algorithmic reasoning trick, but as a learnable metacognitive skill that requires explicit vocabulary building, just like human reflective practice.
